results.possibleIdentity: Julian R. Livingston (1932–2021) was an American electrical engineer and music composer who worked at Bell Telephone Laboratories (Bell Labs) during the 1960s and 1970s. Born in Indiana, he initially pursued music, earning a Bachelor of Music from Indiana University in 1955. After serving as an army officer in Korea (1955–1957), he transitioned to engineering, graduating cum laude with a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from Purdue University and later receiving a Master of Science in Electrical Engineering from the Polytechnic Institute of Brooklyn (now NYU Tandon School of Engineering) in 1966. At Bell Labs in New Jersey, Livingston specialized in data communication research, specifically the transmission of data via modems. His technical work frequently utilized computers for the collection and analysis of modem test data. Outside of his engineering career, Livingston was a prolific composer of operas, chamber music, and choral works; his notable compositions include the opera "A Twist of Treason" (1976), which explored the life of Benedict Arnold. He lived in the Shark River Hills area of New Jersey during his tenure at Bell Labs and eventually retired to Bloomington, Indiana, where he was a dedicated volunteer for the Monroe County History Center and continued his musical and literary pursuits.
